title: gaultheria benomensis var. calvescens
intro: Gaultheria benomensis var. calvescens, also known as Peninsular Heath, is a rare and endemic variety of the Gaultheria benomensis plant found in the Benom region of Malaysia. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its leaves and stems, which contain high concentrations of betulinic acid, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The medicinal properties of Gaultheria benomensis var. calvescens have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Gaultheria benomensis var. calvescens can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are believed to offer health benefits such as immune system support, antioxidant activity, and anti-cancer properties.
